2020-04-25.abstract: Migrant workers globally send hundreds of billions of dollars home every year. The economic paralysis with the coronavirus pandemic threatens that.

2020-04-25.title: Tux, Gown, Masks: Arab Couples Scramble to Marry During a Pandemic
2020-04-25.abstract: Undeterred by the coronavirus, couples across the Middle East are pressing ahead with scaled-back weddings. The authorities aren’t always thrilled.

2020-04-25.title: Bored Russians Posted Silly Art Parodies. The World Has Joined In.
2020-04-25.abstract: Frozen dumplings as skulls and A/C ducts as fashion accessories. A Facebook group for art re-enactors has gained 540,000 followers across the locked-down globe.

2020-04-25.title: 12 Rangers Among 17 Killed in Congo Park Ambush
2020-04-25.abstract: The attack at Virunga National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage site in eastern Congo, was among the deadliest in its recent history.

2020-04-26.title: Global Virus Death Toll Crosses 200,000; Italy to Ease Restrictions in May
2020-04-26.abstract: Boris Johnson is back at Downing Street and will return to work Monday after recovering. Cuba is sending doctors around the world.

2020-04-26.title: Kim Jong-un’s Absence and North Korea’s Silence Keep Rumor Mill Churning
2020-04-26.abstract: Rumors about the North Korean leader’s health — and speculation over his possible death — have only increased over the past two weeks.

2020-04-26.title: U.K. Coronavirus Briefings Offer a More Genteel Spin Than Trump’s
2020-04-26.abstract: Downing Street’s daily updates are worlds away from the White House’s in tone, but they share a purpose: to control the narrative and deflect blame over a heavily criticized response.

2020-04-26.title: Kashmir, Under Siege and Lockdown, Faces a Mental Health Crisis
2020-04-26.abstract: Years of strife left a generation traumatized. India’s clampdown disrupted daily life. Now the battle against the coronavirus has further isolated and scarred a people with little access to help.

2020-04-26.title: Dozens of Gay Men Are Outed in Morocco as Photos Are Spread Online
2020-04-26.abstract: The idea was to show the hypocrisy of Moroccan society by showing how many gay men are living quietly in straight society. It backfired badly.

2020-04-27.title: South Korea Confident That Rumors of Kim Jong-un Illness Are Wrong
2020-04-27.abstract: Comments by the country’s unification minister represent the South’s most categorical rebuttal of reports that the North’s leader may be incapacitated.

2020-04-27.title: Scientists Race For a Coronavirus Vaccine
2020-04-27.abstract: Even as the global death toll passed 200,000, countries are mapping out a return to public life. Health care workers in Mexico, India and other countries are facing attacks.

2020-04-27.title: He Found One of Stalin’s Mass Graves. Now He’s in Jail.
2020-04-27.abstract: The discovery by Yuri Dmitriev, after years of searching, “has clearly made some people very uncomfortable,” his daughter says.

2020-04-27.title: Jakarta’s Trash Mountain: ‘When People Are Desperate for Jobs, They Come Here’
2020-04-27.abstract: The scavengers who make a living picking plastic, metal and even bones from a huge landfill face additional misery as the global economic slowdown closes the recycling centers they count on.

2020-04-27.title: ‘Afraid to Be a Nurse’: Health Workers Under Attack
2020-04-27.abstract: Stigmatized as vectors of contagion in some countries, health care workers have been assaulted, abused and ostracized.

2020-04-28.title: Study Finds Coronavirus in Tiny Airborne Droplets in Wuhan
2020-04-28.abstract: Even when governments don’t force people to stay home, fear does. Spain, France and Greece have announced plans to restart daily life, with caveats.

2020-04-28.title: Peru’s Queen of Quechua Rap Wants to Rescue Indigenous Culture With Her Music
2020-04-28.abstract: Renata Flores, 19, is part of a generation of Peruvian musicians combining the bouncing beats of Latin trap, rap and reggaeton with the sounds, and language, of the Andean countryside.

2020-04-28.title: Unified in Coronavirus Lockdown, India Splinters Over Reopening
2020-04-28.abstract: As India starts loosening restrictions, a once-broad consensus among leaders on how to proceed has weakened — and many people are staying home anyway.

2020-04-28.title: Coronavirus Diplomacy: How China’s Red Cross Serves the Communist Party
2020-04-28.abstract: The Red Cross in China seems like any other humanitarian group. But there is one crucial difference: it is funded and directed by the Chinese Communist Party.

2020-04-28.title: Genoa’s New Bridge Nears Completion, Turning Tragedy Into Hope
2020-04-28.abstract: Nearly two years after 43 people died when a bridge collapsed, its replacement, built in record time, has become a symbol of Italian can-do.

2020-04-29.title: As Coronavirus Deaths Spike, Brazil’s Leader Says, ‘So What?’: Live Updates
2020-04-29.abstract: Fighting fears and travel bans, countries and businesses are trying to rescue summer vacation. At Airbus, signs of trouble emerge.

2020-04-29.title: Pandemic Shakes France’s Faith in a Cornerstone: Strong Central Government
2020-04-29.abstract: Local governments are now challenging the primacy of the centralized state, the foundation of French society, after it allowed supplies of virus-fighting masks and test kits to be depleted.

2020-04-29.title: For Boris Johnson, a Baby Amid a Dizzying Year of Peaks and Valleys
2020-04-29.abstract: The birth of a son follows a sweeping election win, a divorce, an engagement, Brexit and a life-threatening illness during a pandemic.

2020-04-29.title: Fire at Construction Site Kills 38 in South Korea
2020-04-29.abstract: The blaze comes as President Moon Jae-in has struggled to make good on his pledge to put an end to the man-made disasters that have convulsed the country.

2020-04-29.title: ‘This Isn’t the Time for Caviar’: A Chef Finds New Flavors in a Pandemic
2020-04-29.abstract: After the coronavirus lockdown, a chef in Bangkok and his migrant staff started cooking a whole new menu and delivering food to the poor.

2020-04-30.title: As Coronavirus Sickens Mexican Workers, U.S. Presses Their Factories to Stay Open
2020-04-30.abstract: The pandemic threatens to plunge half a billion people into poverty. The U.N. says it has raised just $1 billion of the $90 billion needed to aid poor regions.

2020-04-30.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in May
2020-04-30.abstract: Our picks for May, including ‘Hollywood,’ ‘Upload,’ ‘Billions’ and ‘Hannah Gadsby: Douglas’

2020-04-30.title: Highways of Peru Swell With Families Fleeing Virus
2020-04-30.abstract: Fear of the virus and job loss in cities are pushing many back to the countryside, reversing a decades-long trend of urban migration and raising alarm about a public health crisis in rural areas.

2020-04-30.title: Millions Had Risen Out of Poverty. Coronavirus Is Pulling Them Back.
2020-04-30.abstract: Experts say that for the first time since 1998, global poverty will increase. At least a half billion people could slip into destitution by the end of the year.

2020-04-30.title: Putin, Russia’s Man of Action, Is Passive, Even Bored, in the Coronavirus Era
2020-04-30.abstract: The pandemic has derailed Vladimir V. Putin’s plans for a big military parade and a referendum extending his rule — and now knocked out his prime minister — as the Russian leader struggles to find his stride.
